primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, selon les IFRS, quelle partie détient le pouvoir et donc le contrôle d’une entité lorsqu’il existe plusieurs investisseurs ou parties impliquées, et distinguer ce cas des situations de contrôle conjoint ou de simple influence notable.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the precedence rule strictly: IFRS supersedes IAS, and IFRS 10 directly governs the assessment of whether one investor, rather than another, controls an investee through power, variable returns and linkage, while IFRS 11 and IAS 28 provide distinct alternative models only if individual control is not present.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty: IFRS 10 is the direct governing standard for identifying which investor has power over an investee, while IFRS 11 and IAS 28 remain relevant only as alternative classifications if that control test is not met.

primary_authority.reason: This standard directly governs how to identify whether one investor has power and therefore control over an investee.

supporting_authority.reason: This standard provides the distinct alternative model of joint control when parties must act together and no single investor controls the investee alone.
This standard provides the distinct alternative model of significant influence when neither control nor joint control exists.

peripheral_authority.reason: This standard concerns related party disclosures and does not govern which party has power for consolidation or joint arrangement classification.

treatment_families.family: Individual control assessment based on power, variable returns and the ability to use power to affect returns
Collective control requiring unanimous consent over relevant activities
Ability to participate in financial and operating policy decisions without control or joint control

approaches.label: Control
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 is the primary model for concluding that one investor, rather than another, has power over the investee and therefore controls it.

approaches.label: Joint control
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 11 is the peer alternative when relevant activities require unanimous consent of the parties sharing control, so no single investor controls alone.

approaches.label: Significant influence
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 28 is the further alternative when an investor can participate in policy decisions but does not have control or joint control.
